note: Written by the cloud pipeline (fetch-data.js via GitHub Actions). Values are a real snapshot; the workflow overwrites this on each scheduled run.

banner.title: El Niño — Easing
banner.desc: A strong El Niño, easing slightly, during Malaysia's drier southwest-monsoon season. Expect a warm, dry bias with elevated drought and transboundary-haze risk.

drivers.name: Niño 3.4 SST anomaly
drivers.interp: El Niño in the strong range. For Malaysia: hotter, drier bias.

drivers.name: ONI (3-month running)
drivers.interp: Official 3-month index in moderate El Niño range and rising. ≥ +1.5 = strong; ≥ +2.0 = 'super'.
drivers.src: NOAA CPC ONI · fallback NOAA PSL

drivers.name: SOI (monthly, Troup)
drivers.interp: Deeply negative SOI confirms the atmosphere is locked into El Niño. ≤ −7 = El Niño-consistent.

drivers.name: DMI (Dipole Mode Index)
drivers.interp: Currently neutral, but a POSITIVE IOD is forecast to develop — that would compound El Niño drying over Malaysia. Watch closely.

drivers.name: RMM phase & amplitude
drivers.interp: MJO stalled in the western Pacific under El Niño westerlies — not delivering a wet pulse to Malaysia right now.
drivers.src: Australia BoM RMM · fallback NOAA CPC

regions.label: High risk
regions.p: Driest & most haze-prone zone in SW monsoon. Heat + reduced rainfall; watch Klang Valley/Melaka air quality.

regions.label: Moderate
regions.p: Near-normal to drier now (NE monsoon inactive). Main flood season is Nov–Mar — watch if La Niña returns.

regions.label: Moderate
regions.p: Drier bias; agricultural water stress and localised fire risk possible as El Niño strengthens.

regions.label: High risk
regions.p: Peatland fire & haze risk elevated under prolonged dry El Niño conditions; monitor hotspots.

rules: → Short-term wet pulse overrides seasonal dry
→ Fall back + flag on source panel
